channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: NGOs Allege Rights Abuses by Western States Amid Pandemic Pact Talks
item.description: More than a year after the Pandemic Agreement was adopted, talks drag on at the World Health Organization in Geneva this week over a key annex that would ensure health equity and allow developing countries to share pathogen materials with the understanding that resulting treatments will be shared with them. Frustrated by deadlocked negotiations, which ... Read more

item.title: From the Nuclear Age to the Age of Artificial Intelligence: Can Humanity Build a New Architecture for Peace?
item.description: More than eight decades after the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki ushered humanity into the nuclear age, the world is confronting another technological revolution whose consequences extend far beyond science and industry. Nuclear weapons still possess the capacity to destroy civilization within hours. At the same time, artificial intelligence is transforming military planning, intelligence […]

item.title: UN Raises Alarm Over A Sharp Rise in Human Rights Abuses and Cholera-Related Deaths in Sudan
item.description: Throughout 2026, the humanitarian crisis in Sudan has deteriorated significantly, prompting the United Nations (UN) to raise alarm over the escalation of human rights violations. Persistent clashes between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) continue to cause mass civilian casualties, drive widespread displacement, and obstruct the delivery of life-saving aid. […]

item.title: Women Peacebuilders: The Missing Voices at the Negotiating Table
item.description: For most individuals, the process of peace starts with the signing of a ceasefire or an agreement among politicians. However, those who live in regions experiencing violence understand that peace is made long before politicians meet at the negotiating table. Peace is created among communities by people who work everyday to ensure that no violence […]

item.title: Afghanistan: 3.7 million young lives at risk of malnutrition, UNICEF warns
item.description: Some 3.7 million children under five in Afghanistan are at heightened risk of malnutrition due to food insecurity, poor diets and inadequate access to basic services as the peak season for life-threatening wasting looms.

item.title: Europe’s Funding Question Puts Tanzania’s Fragile Democracy on Trial
item.description: Every evening just before sunset, Salima Kitwana hobbles into her backyard holding a photograph. In the picture, her son Hemedi, wearing a green football jersey, smiles awkwardly into the camera, unaware that his fate would soon be engulfed in one of Tanzania’s darkest political chapters. At 57, Kitwana has lived with diabetes for nine years, […]

item.title: Does India’s Women’s Reservation Bill Shortchange Women Yet Again?
item.description: To say that the men scored over women yet again would be an understatement. To say that the women lost and men have won would be an oversimplification and to say that political manoeuvring, intrigue and deceit outdid half of India’s population would be stating the obvious. So, what is the story? Or the plot […]

item.title: MEXICO: ‘Our Call for Justice for Our Missing Loved Ones Must Reach the World Just as World Cup Goals Do’
item.description: CIVICUS speaks about efforts to use the 2026 FIFA World Cup to highlight Mexico’s enforced disappearance crisis with Ana Enamorado, a Honduran national who continues to search for her missing son in Mexico, and founder of the Regional Network of Migrant Families. Enforced disappearances in Mexico hit migrant families particularly hard, as their precarious […]

item.title: The Next UN Secretary-General Must Break Not Only the Glass Ceiling, but Also the Culture of Patronage
item.description: As the United Nations (UN) Security Council prepares for its first round of closed-door straw polls this month to select the tenth Secretary-General, the organization stands at a critical crossroads. Multilateralism is fracturing under geopolitical gridlock, and the UN is battling a severe budgetary deficit driven by funding cuts. Yet the gravest threat to the […]
